data:
Search results for "llvmlite PassManager optimization O2 O3 example":

Title: Optimization passes - llvmlite - Read the Docs
URL: https://llvmlite.readthedocs.io/en/v0.47.0/binding/passmanager.html
Content: A pass manager for running optimization passes on an LLVM module. ... This example shows how to use the new pass manager to optimize LLVM IR.

Title: [RFC] Making the pass manager aware of function-level optimization ...
URL: https://discourse.llvm.org/t/rfc-making-the-pass-manager-aware-of-function-level-optimization-attributes/73736?page=2
Content: “an optimization level” and O1/O2/O3 are two different things. The former is abstract concept, the latter refers to a very specific “pipeline

Title: [llvm-dev] Is it possible to reproduce the result of opt -O3 manually?
URL: https://groups.google.com/g/llvm-dev/c/heZUV3rIuxk
Content: I am trying to reproduce the results of applying opt -O3 to a source file in the form of LLVM IR. I want to get the same IR by manually ordering the passes

Title: How to register a pass at -O3 optimization level?
URL: https://discourse.llvm.org/t/how-to-register-a-pass-at-o3-optimization-level/60721
Content: I want to register SeparateConstOffsetFromGEPPass at -O3 under new pass manager. I could not understand pass registration under new PM

Title: Making the LLVM Legacy Pass Manager 1.65x Faster :: Neil Henning
URL: https://www.neilhenning.dev/posts/making-legacy-pass-manager-1_65x-faster
Content: One of the things I was going to talk about in my COVID cancelled EuroLLVM 2020 talk was about a neat little trick we used to get the LLVM legacy pass manager to be significantly faster. LLVM has a default pass optimization pipeline that you create with the `PassManagerBuilder` class:. llvm::legacy::PassManager passManager; llvm::legacy::PassManager passManager; ::::   llvm::PassManagerBuilder passManagerBuilder; llvm::PassManagerBuilder passManagerBuilder; ::   // We can set the optimization level (-O0 ..
